Press the center button on the 4x4 selector to turn on or off
Depress the accelerator pedal to speed up
Back off the accelerator with your foot still on the pedal to slow down
Release the pedal completely to come to a complete stop
Put your off-roading skills to the test and follow these guidelines:
**Stay in 4L for max throttle control.** Slow and precise wins the day
**Look and think ahead.** Take the time to choose the correct line through the rocks.
**Modulate the throttle.** Too much or too little can make a big difference.
**Minimize steering inputs.** Subtle movements will get you through
**Keep your tires on top of the rocks.** The path of least resistance.
**Watch your sidewalls.** Sharp rocks can puncture the side of a tire.
If you don't know the dept, dont attemt to cross
Check bottom conditions for traction - is it sand, mud, rocks?
Plan a line diagonal to the current
Enter smoothly to prevent excessive splashing
Maintain steady forward momentum - not too fast and not too slow
Create a bow wave to disperse water away from the engine bay
Getting Tourque to the Wheels with Electronic
Locking differentials force the two wheels of an axle to turn at the same speed when engaged
Locking differentials prevent the wheel without traction from spinning and delivers torque with traction
Should not be left engaged on dry pavement (causes wheel scrubbing and driveline damage
Use when going over large rocks or very uneven terrain
Allows full articulation to help keep the extended wheel on the ground
Hydraulically actuated - the stabilizer bar con disconnect under load
Must be in off-road G.O.A.T. Modes to disconnect - Sand, Mud/Ruts, Rock Crawl, Baja or Normal
Must be at speeds below 20 mph
Automatically disconnects when in Rock Crawl mode
Automaticallly reconnects once back on level ground and at 20 mph
Increases the contact patch for better grip
Be aware of reduced ground clearance
Designed to decrease the risk of a tire dismounting from the wheel when you air down
Beadlocks securely clamp the tire bead between the outer edge of the wheels and a bolt-on bead lock ring
Proceed slowly so you can choose the correct line
Make small changes to steering and throttle when required
Don't know the size of the rock... get out and look!
Trust your spotter to guide you through the correct line
Think ahead and have a plan in case a tire slips off a rock
Know your ground clearance and avoid straddling large rocks
Slowly come forward. Can be used one-handed with other signals, such as steer passenger or driver
Steer passenger or steer driver. Hold this steering input until directed to change it
Try to cross a ditch with one wheel in the ditch at a time
Cross logs at an angle, one wheel at a time, to decrease clearance needed and to maximize traction

Can't make it over and obstacle? Consider using a ramp. If you didn't bring one with you, look around to see if anything is available (rocks or logs) to create a makeshift one. Remember to always Tread Lightly!
7-Speed Manual Transmission (6+1 with Crawler Gear) OR 10-Speed Automatic Transmission with Trail Control
Scout the hill's crest - **don't guess** what's on the other side!
Plan your line parallel to the fall line (the natural path a ball would take rolling down)
Engage Hill Descent Control or Trail Control in case of a failed hill climb
Point the vehicle up the hill and make sure the wheels are straight
Shift into Drive and let the transmission do the work - it will choose the best gear to maintain your momentum
As you reach the top of the hill, ease up on the throttle and slow to a crawl (or stop) to evaluate the descent on the other side
What if I can't make it up the hill?
Stay calm and come to a stop - adjust your steering as needed to direct the vehicle back down the hill
If not already on, engage Trail Control (automatic transmission) or Hill Descent Controls (manual transmission)
Put the vehicle in Reverse and look over your shoulder or use your mirrors as you slowly back down the hill
Don't touch the brake or throttle - Trail Control and Hill Descent Control will manage the rate of descent by applying individual brakes as necessary
Adjust your speed using the SET+ and SET- buttons on the steering wheel
Scout the descent for possible obstacles
Plan your line parallel to the fall line (the natural path a ball would take rolling down). Avoid turning diagonally to the fall line, as this increases the risk of roll over
Point the vehicle down the hill and keep turns minimal
Select manual mode on the shifter and 1st gear (automatic transmission), 4L on the transfer case, for maximum engine braking and control
Maintain a slow, controlled speed - minimize brake useage to reduce the chance of wheel lock up or sliding
Bronco Trail Control & Hill Descent Control
If not already on engage Trail Control (automatic transmission) or Hill Descent Control (manual transmission) and begin your descent

Trail Turn Assist helps manage tight turns on the trail by **reducing the turning radius by as much as 40%**. It does this by applying the brake to the inside rear wheel as the vehicle turns in low-speed off-road environments
How much does the vehicle weigh
How much do onboard passengers and cargo weigh
Are there suspension and / or tire modifications
Consider the vehicles speed and trail conditions
Are there any roof rack loads
If the vehicle starts to slip or roll, turn downhill and lightly accelerate to balance the vehicle. Take a deep breath
ESCV technology was designed to utilize posistion sensitive damping, allowing the passengers to enjoy on-road comfort while maximizing off-road control and stability.
When weight is transferred during a tilt, ESCV provides additional control of the vehicle.
Maintain a steady, quicker pace to **float** the vehicle on top of the sand
Make gentle, wider turns to prevent the tires from digging into the sand
Be smooth with both the throttle and brakes to minimize weight transfer
**Air down** the tires to increase tire footprint
Designed for soft, dry sand (also useful in deep snow)
Optimizes powertrain, steering, and traction control response for these conditions
Engages 4H and locks the rear differential (if equipped) when selected
